tickets.title: US001: Instant Sold Out Toggle
tickets.description:
As a Cafeteria Chef, I want to mark a menu item as 'Sold Out' instantly, so that students cannot continue placing orders for unavailable food.
Acceptance Criteria:
Pass: When the Chef toggles an item to 'Sold Out' on the kitchen screen, the item is updated in real-time on the backend.
Pass: The mobile app immediately blocks students from adding that specific 'Sold Out' item to their cart.

tickets.title: US002: Mobile Food Ordering
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to order food on my mobile phone while walking from class, so that I can avoid waiting in the chaotic 20-minute cafeteria lines.
Acceptance Criteria:
Pass: The student can browse the menu, add items to their cart, and submit a remote order via the mobile interface.
Pass: The system generates a unique order confirmation receipt and estimated pickup time upon successful payment.

tickets.title: US003: Allergen Menu Filtering
tickets.description:
As a Student with a nut allergy, I want to filter the menu to completely hide items containing peanuts, so that I can safely choose my meal without anxiety.
Acceptance Criteria:
Pass: When the "No Peanuts" filter is enabled, the menu immediately hides all items flagged with peanut ingredients (e.g., peanut butter cookies).
Pass: The filter preference is saved to the user's profile and remains active for future sessions until manually disabled.

analysis.ambiguity1: "I want students to order food on their phone while they are walking from class". It lacks specifics regarding order timing and fulfillment. If a student orders while 20 minutes away, will the kitchen make it immediately (resulting in cold food), or does the system require the student to select a specific pick-up time slot? The workflow for managing the preparation timeline is missing.
analysis.ambiguity2: "We want to send push notifications to students at 11:30 AM offering them a discount on the daily special". It is unclear who the target audience for this notification is. Does the system broadcast it to all users with the app installed, or only to users currently geolocated on campus? Additionally, it does not specify if users have the option to opt-out of marketing alerts.
analysis.conflictSolution: The Chef requires the ability to mark items as 'Sold Out' instantly to prevent users from ordering unavailable food. However, the IT Infrastructure Team notes the kitchen Wi-Fi is terrible and the system might go offline for up to 10 minutes. If the kitchen display is offline, the Chef cannot instantly update the mobile app. Implement an automatic network-loss failsafe. If the kitchen display system loses its connection to the main server for more than 30 seconds, the mobile app should automatically pause all incoming orders and display a "System Temporarily Offline" message to students until the connection is restored.
analysis.nfr1: The kitchen display system must be capable of operating offline for at least 10 minutes without crashing and must automatically resynchronize data when the connection is restored.
analysis.nfr2: The system must process and reflect inventory changes (such as marking an item 'Sold Out') instantly across all active mobile app sessions to prevent invalid orders.
analysis.nfr3: The mobile app must provide a strict, reliable filtering mechanism that completely hides menu items containing specific allergens (e.g., nuts) when selected by the user.
